fragility_points: One-minute settlement mechanics amplify transient downside and venue-specific noise risk.
Weekend/liquidity shocks could erase the cushion quickly in crypto.
key_assumptions: No major macro or crypto-specific shock knocks BTC down more than ~4% before settlement.
Binance remains operationally normal and the reference minute is not distorted by venue-specific problems.
main_logical_chain: Settlement is a specific Binance BTC/USDT one-minute close at noon ET on April 20.
Current Binance spot is ~75.1k, already materially above the 72k threshold.
With only four days left and no obvious scheduled macro catalyst in the window, the base case is that BTC remains above the threshold.
Because settlement is path-sensitive to one minute, residual downside shock risk still prevents near-certainty.
main_thesis: BTC is already trading comfortably above 72k and, absent a near-term shock, is likely to stay above that threshold at the April 20 noon ET Binance settlement minute.
quote_anchors: Polymarket rules: resolves to the Binance BTC/USDT 1 minute candle 12:00 ET close.
source_quality_view: High-quality direct contract and pricing sources, plus a weaker but useful independent macro-calendar context check; overall source quality is good enough for a medium-difficulty case.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact 1-minute Binance close, so a brief liquidation or exchange-specific disturbance could still produce a no outcome.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was 75117.76 during the run, leaving ~3118 points of cushion over 72k.
Recent Binance daily closes show BTC has already been holding above 72k across multiple sessions.
No obvious scheduled top-tier Fed catalyst was identified inside the short pre-resolution window.
timing_relevance: This is mainly a four-day catalyst/timing problem: the question is whether any near-term shock can erase a ~4.3% cushion by the exact settlement minute.
unresolved_ambiguities: The Fed calendar extract did not surface a fully explicit event list in-tool, so catalyst absence is checked only to moderate confidence rather than exhaustively proved.
what_would_change_view: A fast move back toward or below 74k, discovery of a high-impact scheduled event before noon ET on April 20, or a Binance-specific operational issue would make me less bullish.
